LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED. PLAINTIFF COULD NO LONGER PERFORM THE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS OF HIS FORMER POSITION AS SEWER MAINTENANCE WORKER EITHER WITH OR WITHOUT AN ACCOMMODATION. PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ABLE TO PERFORM AT LEAST HALF OF THE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS OF A 7215 SUPERVISOR WITHOUT ASSISTANCE FROM SOMEONE ELSE (PLAINTIFF'S DEPO 339-354.) DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN TIMELY, GOOD FAITH INTERACTIVE PROCESS: GRANTED 3 REQUESTS FOR MEDICAL LEAVE, TEMPORARY MODIFIED WORK DUTY, AND EXTENDED SEARCH FOR A SUITABLE OPEN POSITION (UMFS 10, 13-17). NO FAILURE TO MAKE REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION (UMFS 19-20). NO SHOWING OF VACANT POSITIONS FOR WHICH PLAINTIFF QUALIFIED AND CAPABLE OF PERFORMING WITH OR WITHOUT ACCOMMODATION. (UMF 23-24) NO SHOWING OF TEMPORAL PROXIMITY BETWEEN PLAINTIFF'S FILING OF HIS DISCRIMINATION CLAIM AND HIS MEDICAL SEPARATION FROM HIS EMPLOYMENT. (UMF 32) NO SHOWING OF SEVERE AND PERVASIVE HARASSMENT. (UMFS 27-30) CLAIMS OF AIDING, ABETTING, INCITING, COMPELLING, COERCING AND FAILURE TO TAKE REASONABLE STEPS TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION AND HARASSMENT FAIL WITH THE UNDERLYING CLAIMS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Douglas A. Prutton, Esq. of Law Offices of Douglas A. Prutton (510) 704-2960 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Stephanie Bickham, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3827 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment granted. No triable issue of fact raised. Plaintiff could no longer perform the essential functions of his former position as sewer maintenance worker either with or without an accommodation. Plaintiff was not able to perform at least half of the essential functions of a 7215 Supervisor without assistance from someone else (Plaintiff's depo 339-354.) Defendant engaged in timely, good faith interactive process: granted 3 requests for medical leave, temporary modified work duty, and extended search for a suitable open position (UMFs 10, 13-17). No failure to make reasonable accommodation (UMFs 19-20). No showing of vacant positions for which plaintiff qualified and capable of performing with or without accommodation. (UMF 23-24) No showing of temporal proximity between plaintiff's filing of his discrimination claim and his medical separation from his employment. (UMF 32) No showing of severe and pervasive harassment. (UMFs 27-30) Claims of aiding, abetting, inciting, compelling, coercing and failure to take reasonable steps to prevent discrimination and harassment fail with the underlying claims.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
